message.content: The user sees a display in their cc-statusline that looks like "11:34AM/3PM (4h 26m)" and wants to know what it means. The statusline project is at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/cc-statusline/`. Please read the relevant source files to understand what this time display represents and explain it clearly. Focus on what the two times and the duration refer to.

content.text: Let me read the relevant source files to understand this display.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	trap 'echo "ERROR in statusline.sh" >&2; exit 1' ERR
4	input=$(cat)
5	
6	# Configuration file path
7	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
8	CONFIG_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/../config/config.json"
9	
10	# Source utility functions for ccusage_r support (optional)
11	if [ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/statusline-utils.sh" ]; then
12	    source "$SCRIPT_DIR/statusline-utils.sh"
13	fi
14	
15	# Source layer calculation functions
16	if [ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/statusline-layers.sh" ]; then
17	    source "$SCRIPT_DIR/statusline-layers.sh"
18	fi
19	
20	# Source cache management functions
21	if [ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/statusline-cache.sh" ]; then
22	    source "$SCRIPT_DIR/statusline-cache.sh"
23	fi
24	
25	# ====================================================================================
26	# CONFIG VALIDATION
27	# ====================================================================================
28	validate_config() {
29	    local config_json=$1
30	    local errors=()
31	
32	    # Helper function to check if a field exists and is not null
33	    check_required() {
34	        local field=$1
35	        local value=$(echo "$config_json" | jq -r "$field")
36	        if [[ "$value" == "null" || -z "$value" ]]; then
37	            errors+=("Missing required field: $field")
38	            return 1
39	        fi
40	        return 0
41	    }
42	
43	    # Helper function to validate numeric range
44	    check_numeric_range() {
45	        local field=$1
46	        local min=$2
47	        local max=$3
48	        local value=$(echo "$config_json" | jq -r "$field")
49	        if [[ "$value" != "null" && -n "$value" ]]; then
50	            if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($value < $min || $value > $max)}") )); then
51	                errors+=("Field $field=$value is out of range [$min, $max]")
52	            fi
53	        fi
54	    }
55	
56	    # Validate user plan
57	    check_required ".user.plan"
58	    local plan=$(echo "$config_json" | jq -r '.user.plan')
59	    if [[ "$plan" != "pro" && "$plan" != "max5x" && "$plan" != "max20x" ]]; then
60	        errors+=("Invalid user.plan: $plan (must be 'pro', 'max5x', or 'max20x')")
61	    fi
62	
63	    # Validate limits
64	    check_required ".limits.weekly.$plan"
65	    check_required ".limits.cost"
66	    check_required ".limits.context"
67	    check_numeric_range ".limits.cost" 0 1000
68	    check_numeric_range ".limits.context" 0 1000
69	
70	    # Validate multi-layer settings (5-hour window)
71	    check_required ".multi_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier"
72	    check_required ".multi_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier"
73	    check_required ".multi_layer.layer3.threshold_multiplier"
74	    check_numeric_range ".multi_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier" 0 2
75	    check_numeric_range ".multi_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier" 0 2
76	    check_numeric_range ".multi_layer.layer3.threshold_multiplier" 0 5
77	
78	    check_required ".multi_layer.layer1.color"
79	    check_required ".multi_layer.layer2.color"
80	    check_required ".multi_layer.layer3.color"
81	
82	    # Validate daily layer settings
83	    check_required ".daily_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier"
84	    check_required ".daily_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier"
85	    check_numeric_range ".daily_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier" 0 2
86	    check_numeric_range ".daily_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier" 0 5
87	
88	    check_required ".daily_layer.layer1.color"
89	    check_required ".daily_layer.layer2.color"
90	
91	    # Validate context layer settings
92	    check_required ".context_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier"
93	    check_required ".context_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier"
94	    check_required ".context_layer.layer3.threshold_multiplier"
95	    check_numeric_range ".context_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier" 0 5
96	    check_numeric_range ".context_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier" 0 5
97	    check_numeric_range ".context_layer.layer3.threshold_multiplier" 0 5
98	
99	    check_required ".context_layer.layer1.color"
100	    check_required ".context_layer.layer2.color"
101	    check_required ".context_layer.layer3.color"
102	
103	    # Validate display settings
104	    check_required ".display.bar_length"
105	    check_numeric_range ".display.bar_length" 5 50
106	
107	    # Validate weekly scheme
108	    local weekly_scheme=$(echo "$config_json" | jq -r '.tracking.weekly_scheme')
109	    if [[ "$weekly_scheme" != "ccusage" && "$weekly_scheme" != "ccusage_r" && "$weekly_scheme" != "null" ]]; then
110	        errors+=("Invalid tracking.weekly_scheme: $weekly_scheme (must be 'ccusage' or 'ccusage_r')")
111	    fi
112	
113	    # Validate official_reset_date if weekly_scheme is ccusage_r
114	    if [[ "$weekly_scheme" == "ccusage_r" ]]; then
115	        local reset_date=$(echo "$config_json" | jq -r '.tracking.official_reset_date')
116	        if [[ "$reset_date" == "null" || -z "$reset_date" ]]; then
117	            errors+=("tracking.official_reset_date is required when weekly_scheme is 'ccusage_r'")
118	        fi
119	    fi
120	
121	    # Validate payment_cycle_start_date if show_monthly is true
122	    local show_monthly=$(echo "$config_json" | jq -r '.sections.show_monthly')
123	    if [[ "$show_monthly" == "true" ]]; then
124	        local payment_cycle=$(echo "$config_json" | jq -r '.tracking.payment_cycle_start_date')
125	        if [[ "$payment_cycle" == "null" || -z "$payment_cycle" ]]; then
126	            errors+=("tracking.payment_cycle_start_date is required when sections.show_monthly is true")
127	        fi
128	    fi
129	
130	    # Validate color codes (basic check for ANSI format)
131	    local color_names=("orange" "bright_orange" "dim_orange" "red" "dim_red" "pink" "dim_pink" "bright_pink" "green" "dim_green" "purple" "cyan" "dim_blue" "reset")
132	    for color in "${color_names[@]}"; do
133	        check_required ".colors.$color"
134	    done
135	
136	    # Report errors
137	    if [ ${#errors[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
138	        echo "❌ Configuration validation failed:" >&2
139	        for error in "${errors[@]}"; do
140	            echo "  - $error" >&2
141	        done
142	        echo "" >&2
143	        echo "Please check your config file: $CONFIG_FILE" >&2
144	        echo "See config.example.json for reference." >&2
145	        exit 1
146	    fi
147	}
148	
149	# Load configuration from JSON file if it exists
150	if [ -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
151	    CONFIG=$(cat "$CONFIG_FILE")
152	
153	    # Validate configuration before using it
154	    validate_config "$CONFIG"
155	
156	    # User Configuration
157	    USER_PLAN=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.user.plan')
158	
159	    # Limits
160	    WEEKLY_LIMIT_PRO=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.limits.weekly.pro')
161	    WEEKLY_LIMIT_MAX5X=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.limits.weekly.max5x')
162	    WEEKLY_LIMIT_MAX20X=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.limits.weekly.max20x')
163	    CONTEXT_LIMIT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.limits.context')
164	    COST_LIMIT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.limits.cost')
165	    TOKEN_LIMIT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.limits.token')
166	
167	    # Paths
168	    CLAUDE_PROJECTS_PATH=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.paths.claude_projects')
169	
170	    # Display settings
171	    BAR_LENGTH=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.display.bar_length')
172	    TRANSCRIPT_TAIL_LINES=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.display.transcript_tail_lines')
173	    SESSION_ACTIVITY_THRESHOLD=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.display.session_activity_threshold_minutes')
174	    SHOW_LABELS=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .display.show_labels == null then "true" else .display.show_labels | tostring end')
175	
176	    # ccusage version
177	    CCUSAGE_VERSION=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.ccusage_version')
178	
179	    # Multi-layer settings (5-hour window)
180	    LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.multi_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier')
181	    LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.multi_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier')
182	    LAYER3_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.multi_layer.layer3.threshold_multiplier')
183	
184	    LAYER1_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.multi_layer.layer1.color')
185	    LAYER2_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.multi_layer.layer2.color')
186	    LAYER3_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.multi_layer.layer3.color')
187	
188	    # Daily layer settings
189	    DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.daily_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier')
190	    DAILY_LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.daily_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier')
191	
192	    DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.daily_layer.layer1.color')
193	    DAILY_LAYER2_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.daily_layer.layer2.color')
194	
195	    # Context layer settings
196	    CTX_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.context_layer.layer1.threshold_multiplier')
197	    CTX_LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.context_layer.layer2.threshold_multiplier')
198	    CTX_LAYER3_THRESHOLD_MULT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.context_layer.layer3.threshold_multiplier')
199	
200	    CTX_LAYER1_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.context_layer.layer1.color')
201	    CTX_LAYER2_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.context_layer.layer2.color')
202	    CTX_LAYER3_COLOR=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.context_layer.layer3.color')
203	
204	    # Section toggles (use 'if null' to avoid treating false as falsy)
205	    SHOW_DIRECTORY=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_directory == null then "true" else .sections.show_directory | tostring end')
206	    SHOW_CONTEXT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_context == null then "true" else .sections.show_context | tostring end')
207	    SHOW_FIVE_HOUR_WINDOW=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_five_hour_window == null then "true" else .sections.show_five_hour_window | tostring end')
208	    SHOW_DAILY=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_daily == null then "true" else .sections.show_daily | tostring end')
209	    SHOW_WEEKLY=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_weekly == null then "true" else .sections.show_weekly | tostring end')
210	    SHOW_MONTHLY=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_monthly == null then "false" else .sections.show_monthly | tostring end')
211	    SHOW_TIMER=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_timer == null then "true" else .sections.show_timer | tostring end')
212	    SHOW_TOKEN_RATE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_token_rate == null then "true" else .sections.show_token_rate | tostring end')
213	    SHOW_SESSIONS=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r 'if .sections.show_sessions == null then "true" else .sections.show_sessions | tostring end')
214	    WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.sections.weekly_display_mode')
215	
216	    # Tracking settings
217	    WEEKLY_SCHEME=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.tracking.weekly_scheme')
218	    OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.tracking.official_reset_date')
219	    PAYMENT_CYCLE_START_DATE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.tracking.payment_cycle_start_date')
220	    WEEKLY_BASELINE_PCT=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.tracking.weekly_baseline_percent')
221	    CACHE_DURATION=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.tracking.cache_duration_seconds')
222	
223	    # Color codes (trust config is complete, no fallbacks)
224	    ORANGE_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.orange' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
225	    BRIGHT_ORANGE_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.bright_orange' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
226	    DIM_ORANGE_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.dim_orange' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
227	    RED_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.red' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
228	    DIM_RED_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.dim_red' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
229	    PINK_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.pink' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
230	    DIM_PINK_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.dim_pink' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
231	    BRIGHT_PINK_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.bright_pink' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
232	    GREEN_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.green' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
233	    DIM_GREEN_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.dim_green' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
234	    PURPLE_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.purple' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
235	    CYAN_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.cyan' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
236	    DIM_BLUE_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.dim_blue' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
237	    RESET_CODE=$(echo "$CONFIG" | jq -r '.colors.reset' | sed 's/\\\\/\\/g')
238	else
239	    # Default configuration (fallback if config file doesn't exist)
240	    USER_PLAN="max5x"
241	    WEEKLY_LIMIT_PRO=300
242	    WEEKLY_LIMIT_MAX5X=500
243	    WEEKLY_LIMIT_MAX20X=850
244	    CONTEXT_LIMIT=168
245	    COST_LIMIT=140
246	    TOKEN_LIMIT=220000
247	    CLAUDE_PROJECTS_PATH="~/.claude/projects/"
248	    BAR_LENGTH=10
249	    TRANSCRIPT_TAIL_LINES=200
250	    SESSION_ACTIVITY_THRESHOLD=5
251	    SHOW_LABELS="true"
252	    CCUSAGE_VERSION="17.1.0"
253	    LAYER1_THRESHOLD=30
254	    LAYER2_THRESHOLD=50
255	    LAYER3_THRESHOLD=100
256	    LAYER1_COLOR="green"
257	    LAYER2_COLOR="orange"
258	    LAYER3_COLOR="red"
259	    # Calculate multipliers dynamically
260	    LAYER1_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / $LAYER1_THRESHOLD}")
261	    LAYER2_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / ($LAYER2_THRESHOLD - $LAYER1_THRESHOLD)}")
262	    LAYER3_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / ($LAYER3_THRESHOLD - $LAYER2_THRESHOLD)}")
263	    # Default daily layer settings (two-layer system)
264	    DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD=14.29
265	    DAILY_LAYER2_THRESHOLD=21.44
266	    DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR="green"
267	    DAILY_LAYER2_COLOR="orange"
268	    # Calculate daily multipliers dynamically
269	    DAILY_LAYER1_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / $DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD}")
270	    DAILY_LAYER2_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / ($DAILY_LAYER2_THRESHOLD - $DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD)}")
271	    # Default section toggles (all strings for consistency)
272	    SHOW_DIRECTORY="true"
273	    SHOW_CONTEXT="true"
274	    SHOW_FIVE_HOUR_WINDOW="true"
275	    SHOW_DAILY="true"
276	    SHOW_WEEKLY="true"
277	    SHOW_MONTHLY="false"
278	    SHOW_TIMER="true"
279	    SHOW_TOKEN_RATE="true"
280	    SHOW_SESSIONS="true"
281	    # Default tracking settings
282	    WEEKLY_BASELINE_PCT=0
283	    CACHE_DURATION=300
284	    PAYMENT_CYCLE_START_DATE=""
285	
286	    # Default color codes
287	    ORANGE_CODE='\033[1;93m'
288	    BRIGHT_ORANGE_CODE='\033[38;5;208m'
289	    DIM_ORANGE_CODE='\033[2;38;5;208m'
290	    RED_CODE='\033[31m'
291	    DIM_RED_CODE='\033[2;31m'
292	    PINK_CODE='\033[38;5;225m'
293	    DIM_PINK_CODE='\033[2;38;5;225m'
294	    BRIGHT_PINK_CODE='\033[1;38;5;225m'
295	    GREEN_CODE='\033[38;5;194m'
296	    DIM_GREEN_CODE='\033[2;38;5;194m'
297	    PURPLE_CODE='\033[35m'
298	    CYAN_CODE='\033[96m'
299	    DIM_BLUE_CODE='\033[94m'
300	    RESET_CODE='\033[0m'
301	
302	    # Default layer multipliers
303	    LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT=0.3
304	    LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT=0.5
305	    LAYER3_THRESHOLD_MULT=1.0
306	
307	    DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT=1.0
308	    DAILY_LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT=1.5
309	
310	    CTX_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT=1.0
311	    CTX_LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT=2.0
312	    CTX_LAYER3_THRESHOLD_MULT=3.0
313	
314	    # Default layer colors
315	    LAYER1_COLOR="green"
316	    LAYER2_COLOR="orange"
317	    LAYER3_COLOR="red"
318	
319	    DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR="green"
320	    DAILY_LAYER2_COLOR="orange"
321	
322	    CTX_LAYER1_COLOR="dim_pink"
323	    CTX_LAYER2_COLOR="dim_orange"
324	    CTX_LAYER3_COLOR="dim_red"
325	fi
326	
327	# Helper function: map color name to ANSI code
328	get_color_code() {
329	    case "$1" in
330	        "orange") echo "$ORANGE_CODE" ;;
331	        "bright_orange") echo "$BRIGHT_ORANGE_CODE" ;;
332	        "dim_orange") echo "$DIM_ORANGE_CODE" ;;
333	        "red") echo "$RED_CODE" ;;
334	        "dim_red") echo "$DIM_RED_CODE" ;;
335	        "pink") echo "$PINK_CODE" ;;
336	        "dim_pink") echo "$DIM_PINK_CODE" ;;
337	        "bright_pink") echo "$BRIGHT_PINK_CODE" ;;
338	        "green") echo "$GREEN_CODE" ;;
339	        "dim_green") echo "$DIM_GREEN_CODE" ;;
340	        "purple") echo "$PURPLE_CODE" ;;
341	        "cyan") echo "$CYAN_CODE" ;;
342	        *) echo "$GREEN_CODE" ;;
343	    esac
344	}
345	
346	# Determine weekly limit based on plan
347	case "$USER_PLAN" in
348	    "pro")
349	        WEEKLY_LIMIT=$WEEKLY_LIMIT_PRO
350	        ;;
351	    "max5x")
352	        WEEKLY_LIMIT=$WEEKLY_LIMIT_MAX5X
353	        ;;
354	    "max20x")
355	        WEEKLY_LIMIT=$WEEKLY_LIMIT_MAX20X
356	        ;;
357	    *)
358	        WEEKLY_LIMIT=$WEEKLY_LIMIT_MAX5X  # Default fallback
359	        ;;
360	esac
361	
362	# ====================================================================================
363	# CACHE DEPENDENCY VALIDATION
364	# ====================================================================================
365	# Validate that cached data dependencies haven't changed
366	# If weekly_limit or weekly_baseline_pct changed, invalidate all caches
367	if type check_and_update_cache_deps &>/dev/null; then
368	    check_and_update_cache_deps "$WEEKLY_LIMIT" "$WEEKLY_BASELINE_PCT"
369	fi
370	
371	# ====================================================================================
372	# THREE-STAGE PIPELINE ARCHITECTURE
373	# ====================================================================================
374	# This statusline follows a three-stage pipeline for data flow:
375	#
376	# STAGE 1: DATA COLLECTION
377	#   - Parse input (workspace, transcript_path)
378	#   - Fetch external data (ccusage, transcript tokens, timestamps)
379	#   - All external process calls happen here
380	#   - Conditional: only collect data for enabled sections
381	#
382	# STAGE 2: COMPUTATION
383	#   - Calculate derived metrics (percentages, layers, projections)
384	#   - Determine visual properties (colors, filled blocks)
385	#   - Apply layer calculation functions
386	#   - No external calls, pure computation on collected data
387	#
388	# STAGE 3: RENDERING
389	#   - Build progress bars and formatted strings
390	#   - Assemble statusline sections
391	#   - Apply colors and formatting codes
392	#   - Output final statusline string
393	#
394	# Benefits:
395	#   - Clear separation of concerns
396	#   - Easier to test individual stages
397	#   - Obvious data dependencies
398	#   - Conditional evaluation for performance
399	# ====================================================================================
400	
401	# ====================================================================================
402	# STAGE 1: DATA COLLECTION
403	# ====================================================================================
404	
405	# Extract basic information from JSON input
406	CURRENT_DIR=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // "~"')
407	DIR_NAME="${CURRENT_DIR##*/}"
408	# Sanitize DIR_NAME to prevent ANSI injection
409	DIR_NAME=$(printf '%s' "$DIR_NAME" | tr -d '\000-\037\177')
410	TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
411	
412	# Extract and shorten model name: eu.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 → sonnet 4.6
413	MODEL_RAW=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.id // .model // ""')
414	if [ -n "$MODEL_RAW" ]; then
415	    # Strip vendor prefix (eu.anthropic. or anthropic.)
416	    MODEL_SHORT="${MODEL_RAW##*.claude-}"
417	    MODEL_SHORT="${MODEL_SHORT##*claude-}"
418	    # Convert trailing date suffix on haiku (e.g. haiku-4-5-20251001 → haiku-4-5)
419	    MODEL_SHORT=$(echo "$MODEL_SHORT" | sed 's/-[0-9]\{8\}$//')
420	    # Replace last hyphen separator between version parts with dot (sonnet-4-6 → sonnet-4.6)
421	    MODEL_SHORT=$(echo "$MODEL_SHORT" | sed 's/-\([0-9]\)-\([0-9]\)$/-\1.\2/')
422	fi
423	
424	# Get 5-hour window data from ccusage (needed by 5-HOUR WINDOW, TIMER, and/or TOKEN_RATE sections)
425	# Only fetch if at least one of these sections is enabled
426	if [ "$SHOW_FIVE_HOUR_WINDOW" = "true" ] || [ "$SHOW_TIMER" = "true" ] || [ "$SHOW_TOKEN_RATE" = "true" ]; then
427	    # Use --offline for faster execution with cached pricing
428	    # Filter out npm warnings and capture only the JSON
429	    WINDOW_DATA=$(cd ~ && npx --yes "ccusage@${CCUSAGE_VERSION}" blocks --active --json --token-limit $TOKEN_LIMIT --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
430	
431	    if [ -n "$WINDOW_DATA" ] && [ "$WINDOW_DATA" != "null" ]; then
432	        # Parse window data
433	        BLOCK=$(echo "$WINDOW_DATA" | jq -r '.blocks[0] // empty')
434	
435	        if [ -n "$BLOCK" ]; then
436	            # Extract cost and projection data (needed by daily projection)
437	            COST=$(echo "$BLOCK" | jq -r '.costUSD // 0')
438	
439	            # Extract burn rate data for more accurate projection
440	            COST_PER_HOUR=$(echo "$BLOCK" | jq -r '.burnRate.costPerHour // 0')
441	            REMAINING_MINUTES=$(echo "$BLOCK" | jq -r '.projection.remainingMinutes // 0')
442	            TOKENS_PER_MINUTE=$(echo "$BLOCK" | jq -r '.burnRate.tokensPerMinuteForIndicator // 0')
443	
444	            # Calculate burn-rate based projection (more accurate for recent activity)
445	            # Formula: current_cost + (burn_rate × remaining_time)
446	            if [ "$COST_PER_HOUR" != "0" ] && [ "$REMAINING_MINUTES" != "0" ]; then
447	                PROJECTED_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $COST + ($COST_PER_HOUR * $REMAINING_MINUTES / 60)}")
448	            else
449	                # Fallback to ccusage's linear projection if burn rate unavailable
450	                PROJECTED_COST=$(echo "$BLOCK" | jq -r '.projection.totalCost // 0')
451	            fi
452	
453	            # ========================================================================
454	            # 5-HOUR WINDOW SECTION (conditional display)
455	            # ========================================================================
456	            if [ "$SHOW_FIVE_HOUR_WINDOW" = "true" ]; then
457	
458	                # --- STAGE 2: COMPUTATION ---
459	                # Calculate actual percentage
460	                ACTUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($COST / $COST_LIMIT) * 100}")
461	
462	                # Calculate layer metrics using generic function (pass raw cost in dollars)
463	                LAYER_RESULT=$(calculate_three_layer_metrics \
464	                    "$COST" \
465	                    "$COST_LIMIT" \
466	                    "$LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
467	                    "$LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
468	                    "$LAYER3_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
469	                    "$LAYER1_COLOR" \
470	                    "$LAYER2_COLOR" \
471	                    "$LAYER3_COLOR" \
472	                    "$BAR_LENGTH")
473	                IFS='|' read -r LAYER_NUM VISUAL_PCT BAR_COLOR FILLED <<< "$LAYER_RESULT"
474	
475	                # Calculate layer thresholds and multipliers (needed for projection logic below)
476	                LAYER1_THRESHOLD=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $COST_LIMIT * $LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
477	                LAYER2_THRESHOLD=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $COST_LIMIT * $LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
478	                LAYER3_THRESHOLD=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $COST_LIMIT * $LAYER3_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
479	                LAYER1_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / $LAYER1_THRESHOLD}")
480	                LAYER2_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / ($LAYER2_THRESHOLD - $LAYER1_THRESHOLD)}")
481	                LAYER3_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / ($LAYER3_THRESHOLD - $LAYER2_THRESHOLD)}")
482	
483	                # Calculate projected position using CURRENT layer's multiplier for consistent scale
484	                PROJECTED_POS=-1
485	                PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR="$LAYER1_COLOR"
486	                if [ -n "$PROJECTED_COST" ] && [ "$PROJECTED_COST" != "0" ]; then
487	                    PROJECTED_ACTUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($PROJECTED_COST / $COST_LIMIT) * 100}")
488	
489	                    # Determine projection color based on which layer it falls into (dollar values)
490	                    if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($PROJECTED_COST <= $LAYER1_THRESHOLD)}") )); then
491	                        PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR="$LAYER1_COLOR"
492	                    elif (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($PROJECTED_COST <= $LAYER2_THRESHOLD)}") )); then
493	                        PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR="$LAYER2_COLOR"
494	                    else
495	                        PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR="$LAYER3_COLOR"
496	                    fi
497	
498	                    # Calculate visual position using CURRENT layer's multiplier (same scale as current bar)
499	                    if [ "$BAR_COLOR" = "$LAYER1_COLOR" ]; then
500	                        PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $PROJECTED_COST * $LAYER1_MULTIPLIER}")
501	                    elif [ "$BAR_COLOR" = "$LAYER2_COLOR" ]; then
502	                        PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($PROJECTED_COST - $LAYER1_THRESHOLD) * $LAYER2_MULTIPLIER}")
503	                    else
504	                        PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($PROJECTED_COST - $LAYER2_THRESHOLD) * $LAYER3_MULTIPLIER}")
505	                    fi
506	
507	                    if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT > 100)}") )); then
508	                        PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT=100
509	                    fi
510	
511	                    PROJECTED_POS=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", ($PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT / 100) * $BAR_LENGTH}")
512	                    if [ $PROJECTED_POS -gt $BAR_LENGTH ]; then
513	                        PROJECTED_POS=$BAR_LENGTH
514	                    fi
515	
516	                    # Don't show separator if it's at same position as current
517	                    if [ $PROJECTED_POS -eq $FILLED ]; then
518	                        PROJECTED_POS=-1
519	                    fi
520	                fi
521	
522	                # --- STAGE 3: RENDERING ---
523	                # Set projected separator color from config
524	                PROJECTED_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR")
525	
526	                # Set current progress bar color from config
527	                CURRENT_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$BAR_COLOR")
528	
529	                # Build progress bar with colored projection separator
530	                PROGRESS_BAR="["
531	                for ((i=0; i<BAR_LENGTH; i++)); do
532	                    if [ $i -lt $FILLED ]; then
533	                        PROGRESS_BAR="${PROGRESS_BAR}█"
534	                    elif [ $i -eq $PROJECTED_POS ]; then
535	                        # Projection separator uses current layer color (displayed on current bar)
536	                        PROGRESS_BAR="${PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}${CURRENT_COLOR}│${RESET_CODE}${CURRENT_COLOR}"
537	                    else
538	                        PROGRESS_BAR="${PROGRESS_BAR}░"
539	                    fi
540	                done
541	
542	                # Handle separator at end position (when PROJECTED_POS == BAR_LENGTH)
543	                # This occurs when projection crosses layer boundary and is capped
544	                if [ $PROJECTED_POS -eq $BAR_LENGTH ]; then
545	                    PROGRESS_BAR="${PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}${CURRENT_COLOR}│${RESET_CODE}${CURRENT_COLOR}"
546	                fi
547	
548	                PROGRESS_BAR="${PROGRESS_BAR}]"
549	
550	                # Calculate cost percentage
551	                COST_PERCENTAGE=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", ($COST / $COST_LIMIT) * 100}")
552	
553	                # Format cost
554	                COST_FMT=$(printf "\$%.0f/\$%d" $COST $COST_LIMIT)
555	
556	                # Set progress bar color based on layer
557	                PROGRESS_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$BAR_COLOR")
558	            fi
559	
560	            # ========================================================================
561	            # TIMER SECTION (conditional)
562	            # ========================================================================
563	            if [ "$SHOW_TIMER" = "true" ]; then
564	                # Extract time data
565	                REMAINING_MINS=$(echo "$BLOCK" | jq -r '.projection.remainingMinutes // 0')
566	                END_TIME=$(echo "$BLOCK" | jq -r '.endTime // ""')
567	
568	                # Format countdown
569	                HOURS=$((REMAINING_MINS / 60))
570	                MINS=$((REMAINING_MINS % 60))
571	                TIME_LEFT="${MINS}m"
572	                if [ $HOURS -gt 0 ]; then
573	                    TIME_LEFT="${HOURS}h ${MINS}m"
574	                fi
575	
576	                # Get current time with minutes (format: 5:45PM)
577	                CURRENT_TIME=$(date "+%-l:%M%p" 2>/dev/null || date "+%I:%M%p" | sed 's/^0//')
578	
579	                # Format reset time (simplified format: 10PM - no minutes)
580	                if [ -n "$END_TIME" ]; then
581	                    # Try GNU date first (Linux), then macOS date
582	                    RESET_TIME=$(date -d "$END_TIME" "+%-l%p" 2>/dev/null) || true
583	                    if [ -z "$RESET_TIME" ]; then
584	                        # Fallback to macOS date
585	                        END_TIME_CLEAN=$(echo "$END_TIME" | sed 's/\.[0-9]*Z$//')
586	                        RESET_TIME=$(date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$END_TIME_CLEAN" "+%-l%p" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
587	                    fi
588	                else
589	                    RESET_TIME=""
590	                fi
591	
592	                # Dim color for secondary info (50% opacity effect)
593	                DIM_CODE="\033[2m"
594	
595	                # Format reset info: [current]/[reset] (remaining) with dimmed secondary parts
596	                if [ -n "$RESET_TIME" ]; then
597	                    RESET_INFO="$CURRENT_TIME${DIM_CODE}/$RESET_TIME ($TIME_LEFT)${RESET_CODE}"
598	                else
599	                    RESET_INFO="$TIME_LEFT"
600	                fi
601	            fi
602	
603	            # ========================================================================
604	            # TOKEN RATE SECTION (conditional)
605	            # ========================================================================
606	            if [ "$SHOW_TOKEN_RATE" = "true" ]; then
607	                # TOKENS_PER_MINUTE already extracted above (billable tokens only)
608	                if [ -n "$TOKENS_PER_MINUTE" ] && [ "$TOKENS_PER_MINUTE" != "0" ]; then
609	                    # Format token rate (e.g., 928/min or 1.2k/min)
610	                    if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($TOKENS_PER_MINUTE >= 1000)}") )); then
611	                        # Display in thousands for large rates
612	                        TOKEN_RATE_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.1fk\", $TOKENS_PER_MINUTE / 1000}")
613	                    else
614	                        # Display as integer for small rates
615	                        TOKEN_RATE_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $TOKENS_PER_MINUTE}")
616	                    fi
617	                    TOKEN_RATE="${TOKEN_RATE_DISPLAY}/min"
618	                fi
619	            fi
620	        fi
621	    fi
622	fi
623	
624	# ====================================================================================
625	# CONTEXT SECTION (independent)
626	# ====================================================================================
627	if [ "$SHOW_CONTEXT" = "true" ] && [ -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
628	    # Calculate context window usage from transcript
629	    # Use ccusage method: latest assistant message only
630	    # Separate cached (system overhead) vs fresh (conversation) context
631	    # Get the LATEST assistant message (last N lines for performance)
632	    # Extract token types and calculate cached vs fresh
633	    TOKEN_DATA=$(tail -$TRANSCRIPT_TAIL_LINES "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" | \
634	        grep '"role":"assistant"' | \
635	        tail -1 | \
636	        awk '
637	        {
638	            input = 0
639	            cache_creation = 0
640	            cache_read = 0
641	
642	            # Extract input_tokens (fresh conversation)
643	            if (match($0, /"input_tokens":[0-9]+/)) {
644	                input = substr($0, RSTART, RLENGTH)
645	                gsub(/.*:/, "", input)
646	            }
647	
648	            # Extract cache_creation_input_tokens (cached)
649	            if (match($0, /"cache_creation_input_tokens":[0-9]+/)) {
650	                cache_creation = substr($0, RSTART, RLENGTH)
651	                gsub(/.*:/, "", cache_creation)
652	            }
653	
654	            # Extract cache_read_input_tokens (cached)
655	            if (match($0, /"cache_read_input_tokens":[0-9]+/)) {
656	                cache_read = substr($0, RSTART, RLENGTH)
657	                gsub(/.*:/, "", cache_read)
658	            }
659	
660	            # Cached = cache_creation + cache_read (system overhead)
661	            cached = cache_creation + cache_read
662	            # Fresh = input_tokens (active conversation)
663	            fresh = input
664	            # Total context
665	            total = cached + fresh
666	
667	            # Output: cached(k) fresh(k) total(k)
668	            print int(cached / 1000) " " int(fresh / 1000) " " int(total / 1000)
669	        }')
670	
671	    # Parse the output
672	    CACHED_TOKENS=$(echo "$TOKEN_DATA" | awk '{print $1}')
673	    FRESH_TOKENS=$(echo "$TOKEN_DATA" | awk '{print $2}')
674	    CONTEXT_TOKENS=$(echo "$TOKEN_DATA" | awk '{print $3}')
675	
676	    # Fallback if no data found
677	    if [ -z "$CONTEXT_TOKENS" ]; then
678	        CACHED_TOKENS=0
679	        FRESH_TOKENS=0
680	        CONTEXT_TOKENS=0
681	    fi
682	
683	    # Calculate layer metrics using generic function
684	    CTX_LAYER_RESULT=$(calculate_three_layer_metrics \
685	        "$CONTEXT_TOKENS" \
686	        "$CONTEXT_LIMIT" \
687	        "$CTX_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
688	        "$CTX_LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
689	        "$CTX_LAYER3_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
690	        "$CTX_LAYER1_COLOR" \
691	        "$CTX_LAYER2_COLOR" \
692	        "$CTX_LAYER3_COLOR" \
693	        "$BAR_LENGTH")
694	    IFS='|' read -r CTX_LAYER_NUM CTX_VISUAL_PCT CTX_COLOR_NAME CTX_FILLED <<< "$CTX_LAYER_RESULT"
695	
696	    # Get color code from color name
697	    CTX_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$CTX_COLOR_NAME")
698	
699	    # Create context progress bar (same length as cost bar)
700	    CTX_BAR_LENGTH=$BAR_LENGTH
701	
702	    CTX_EMPTY=$((CTX_BAR_LENGTH - CTX_FILLED))
703	
704	    # Build progress bar
705	    CTX_PROGRESS_BAR="["
706	
707	    for ((i=0; i<CTX_FILLED; i++)); do
708	        CTX_PROGRESS_BAR="${CTX_PROGRESS_BAR}█"
709	    done
710	
711	    for ((i=0; i<CTX_EMPTY; i++)); do
712	        CTX_PROGRESS_BAR="${CTX_PROGRESS_BAR}░"
713	    done
714	
715	    CTX_PROGRESS_BAR="${CTX_PROGRESS_BAR}]"
716	
717	    # Format context - separate total from breakdown
718	    CTX_TOTAL=$(printf "%dk/%dk" $CONTEXT_TOKENS $CONTEXT_LIMIT)
719	    CTX_BREAKDOWN=$(printf "%dk+%dk" $CACHED_TOKENS $FRESH_TOKENS)
720	fi
721	
722	# ====================================================================================
723	# SHARED WEEKLY/DAILY SECTION DATA (integrated weekly tracker)
724	# ====================================================================================
725	if [ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" = "true" ] || [ "$SHOW_DAILY" = "true" ]; then
726	    # Get weekly usage based on configured tracking scheme (shared data source)
727	    if [ "$WEEKLY_SCHEME" = "ccusage_r" ] && [ -n "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE" ] && type get_official_weekly_cost &>/dev/null; then
728	        # Use ccusage costs filtered by official Anthropic reset schedule
729	        # Convert ISO timestamp to Unix format
730	        RESET_TIMESTAMP=$(iso_to_timestamp "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE")
731	        WEEK_COST_RAW=$(get_official_weekly_cost "$RESET_TIMESTAMP" "$CACHE_DURATION")
732	    else
733	        # Use ccusage with ISO weeks (default)
734	        WEEKLY_DATA=$(cd ~ && npx --yes "ccusage@${CCUSAGE_VERSION}" weekly --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
735	        WEEK_COST_RAW=$(echo "$WEEKLY_DATA" | jq -r '.weekly[-1].totalCost // 0')
736	    fi
737	
738	    # Save raw cost for recommendation calculation (without baseline)
739	    WEEK_COST=$WEEK_COST_RAW
740	
741	    # Apply baseline offset to account for untracked costs (for display only)
742	    if [ "$(awk "BEGIN {print ($WEEKLY_BASELINE_PCT != 0)}")" = "1" ]; then
743	        BASELINE_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($WEEKLY_LIMIT * $WEEKLY_BASELINE_PCT) / 100}")
744	        WEEK_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $WEEK_COST + $BASELINE_COST}")
745	    fi
746	
747	    WEEKLY_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", ($WEEK_COST / $WEEKLY_LIMIT) * 100}")
748	fi
749	
750	# ====================================================================================
751	# WEEKLY_DAILY_TRACKER (daily subsection - shows daily usage)
752	# ====================================================================================
753	# Calculate daily cost if daily is shown OR if weekly recommend mode needs it
754	if [ -n "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE" ] && type get_daily_cost &>/dev/null; then
755	    if [ "$SHOW_DAILY" = "true" ] || ([ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" = "true" ] && [ "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" = "recommend" ]); then
756	        # Use daily cost tracking based on official reset time
757	        # Convert ISO timestamp to Unix format (reuse RESET_TIMESTAMP if already computed)
758	        if [ -z "${RESET_TIMESTAMP:-}" ]; then
759	            RESET_TIMESTAMP=$(iso_to_timestamp "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE")
760	        fi
761	        DAILY_COST=$(get_daily_cost "$RESET_TIMESTAMP" "$CACHE_DURATION")
762	
763	        # Calculate daily percentage (needed for display and/or recommend calculation)
764	        DAILY_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($DAILY_COST / $WEEKLY_LIMIT) * 100}")
765	    fi
766	fi
767	
768	# Build daily visualization (only if SHOW_DAILY is enabled)
769	if [ "$SHOW_DAILY" = "true" ] && [ -n "${DAILY_COST:-}" ]; then
770	
771	    # Calculate projection to end of current 5-hour window
772	    # Formula: daily_cost - current_window_cost + projected_window_cost
773	    # This gives us: accumulated daily usage + what the current session will add
774	    if [ -n "${COST:-}" ] && [ -n "${PROJECTED_COST:-}" ]; then
775	        # We have 5-hour window data available
776	        DAILY_PROJECTED_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $DAILY_COST - $COST + $PROJECTED_COST}")
777	    else
778	        # No active 5-hour window, daily projection = current daily cost
779	        DAILY_PROJECTED_COST=$DAILY_COST
780	    fi
781	
782	    # Calculate daily base threshold and layer thresholds
783	    if [ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" = "true" ] && [ "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" = "recommend" ] && [ -n "${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE:-}" ]; then
784	        # Dynamic mode: base = recommend value
785	        DAILY_BASE_THRESHOLD=$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE
786	    else
787	        # Static mode: base = weekly_limit / 7
788	        DAILY_BASE_THRESHOLD=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($WEEKLY_LIMIT / 7.0) / $WEEKLY_LIMIT * 100}")
789	    fi
790	
791	    # Calculate layer metrics using generic function
792	    DAILY_LAYER_RESULT=$(calculate_two_layer_metrics \
793	        "$DAILY_PCT" \
794	        "$DAILY_BASE_THRESHOLD" \
795	        "$DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
796	        "$DAILY_LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT" \
797	        "$DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR" \
798	        "$DAILY_LAYER2_COLOR" \
799	        "$BAR_LENGTH")
800	    IFS='|' read -r DAILY_LAYER_NUM DAILY_VISUAL_PCT DAILY_BAR_COLOR DAILY_FILLED <<< "$DAILY_LAYER_RESULT"
801	
802	    # Calculate layer thresholds and multipliers (needed for projection logic below)
803	    DAILY_USE_LAYER1_THRESHOLD=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $DAILY_BASE_THRESHOLD * $DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
804	    DAILY_USE_LAYER2_THRESHOLD=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $DAILY_BASE_THRESHOLD * $DAILY_LAYER2_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
805	    DAILY_USE_LAYER1_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / $DAILY_USE_LAYER1_THRESHOLD}")
806	    DAILY_USE_LAYER2_MULTIPLIER=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", 100 / ($DAILY_USE_LAYER2_THRESHOLD - $DAILY_USE_LAYER1_THRESHOLD)}")
807	
808	    # Calculate projected position using CURRENT layer's multiplier for consistent scale
809	    DAILY_PROJECTED_POS=-1
810	    DAILY_PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR="$DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR"
811	    if [ -n "$DAILY_PROJECTED_COST" ] && [ "$DAILY_PROJECTED_COST" != "0" ]; then
812	        DAILY_PROJECTED_ACTUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($DAILY_PROJECTED_COST / $WEEKLY_LIMIT) * 100}")
813	
814	        # Determine projection color based on which layer it falls into
815	        if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($DAILY_PROJECTED_ACTUAL_PCT <= $DAILY_USE_LAYER1_THRESHOLD)}") )); then
816	            DAILY_PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR="$DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR"
817	        else
818	            DAILY_PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR="$DAILY_LAYER2_COLOR"
819	        fi
820	
821	        # Calculate visual position using CURRENT layer's multiplier (same scale as current bar)
822	        if [ "$DAILY_BAR_COLOR" = "$DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR" ]; then
823	            DAILY_PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $DAILY_PROJECTED_ACTUAL_PCT * $DAILY_USE_LAYER1_MULTIPLIER}")
824	        else
825	            DAILY_PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($DAILY_PROJECTED_ACTUAL_PCT - $DAILY_USE_LAYER1_THRESHOLD) * $DAILY_USE_LAYER2_MULTIPLIER}")
826	        fi
827	
828	        if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($DAILY_PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT > 100)}") )); then
829	            DAILY_PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT=100
830	        fi
831	
832	        DAILY_PROJECTED_POS=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", ($DAILY_PROJECTED_VISUAL_PCT / 100) * $BAR_LENGTH}")
833	        if [ $DAILY_PROJECTED_POS -gt $BAR_LENGTH ]; then
834	            DAILY_PROJECTED_POS=$BAR_LENGTH
835	        fi
836	
837	        # Don't show separator if projection equals current cost (no actual projection)
838	        # Use cost comparison instead of position to handle rounding cases
839	        if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($DAILY_PROJECTED_COST == $DAILY_COST)}") )); then
840	            DAILY_PROJECTED_POS=-1
841	        fi
842	    fi
843	
844	    # Set projected separator color from config
845	    DAILY_PROJECTED_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_PROJECTED_BAR_COLOR")
846	
847	    # Set current progress bar color from config
848	    DAILY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_BAR_COLOR")
849	
850	    # Build progress bar with colored projection separator
851	    DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="["
852	    for ((i=0; i<BAR_LENGTH; i++)); do
853	        if [ $i -lt $DAILY_FILLED ]; then
854	            DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}█"
855	        elif [ $i -eq $DAILY_PROJECTED_POS ]; then
856	            # Projection separator uses current layer color (displayed on current bar)
857	            DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}│${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}"
858	        else
859	            DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}░"
860	        fi
861	    done
862	
863	    # Handle separator at end position (when DAILY_PROJECTED_POS == BAR_LENGTH)
864	    # This occurs when projection crosses layer boundary and is capped
865	    if [ $DAILY_PROJECTED_POS -eq $BAR_LENGTH ]; then
866	        DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}│${RESET_CODE}${DAILY_COLOR}"
867	    fi
868	
869	    DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR="${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR}]"
870	
871	    # Format daily percentage for display (rounded to whole number)
872	    DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $DAILY_PCT}")
873	fi
874	
875	# ====================================================================================
876	# WEEKLY_TRACKER (weekly subsection - shows weekly usage/avail/recommend)
877	# ====================================================================================
878	if [ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" = "true" ] && [ -n "${WEEKLY_PCT:-}" ]; then
879	    # Calculate display value and label based on mode
880	    case "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" in
881	        "avail")
882	            WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", 100 - $WEEKLY_PCT}")
883	            WEEKLY_LABEL="avail"
884	            ;;
885	        "recommend")
886	            # Calculate recommended daily usage for stable budgeting
887	            # Formula: (weekly_limit - usage_from_weekly_start_to_daily_cycle_start) / cycles_left
888	            # This gives budget available at current daily cycle start (e.g., 3pm today)
889	            # Stays stable throughout the day, updates only at daily cycle reset
890	
891	            if [ -z "${RESET_TIMESTAMP:-}" ] && [ -n "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE" ] && type iso_to_timestamp &>/dev/null; then
892	                RESET_TIMESTAMP=$(iso_to_timestamp "$OFFICIAL_RESET_DATE")
893	            fi
894	
895	            if [ -n "${RESET_TIMESTAMP:-}" ] && type get_anthropic_period &>/dev/null && type get_daily_period &>/dev/null; then
896	                # Get weekly and daily period boundaries
897	                PERIOD_DATA=$(get_anthropic_period "$RESET_TIMESTAMP")
898	                WEEKLY_START=$(echo "$PERIOD_DATA" | jq -r '.start')
899	                NEXT_RESET=$(echo "$PERIOD_DATA" | jq -r '.end')
900	
901	                DAILY_PERIOD=$(get_daily_period "$RESET_TIMESTAMP")
902	                DAILY_CYCLE_START=$(echo "$DAILY_PERIOD" | jq -r '.start')
903	
904	                # Calculate usage from weekly start to current daily cycle start
905	                # This captures all completed daily cycles in the week
906	                WEEKLY_START_ISO=$(timestamp_to_iso "$WEEKLY_START")
907	                DAILY_CYCLE_START_ISO=$(timestamp_to_iso "$DAILY_CYCLE_START")
908	
909	                # Query ccusage blocks for costs between weekly start and daily cycle start
910	                BLOCKS_DATA=$(cd ~ && npx --yes ccusage blocks --json --offline 2>/dev/null | awk '/^{/,0')
911	
912	                if [ -n "$BLOCKS_DATA" ] && [ "$BLOCKS_DATA" != "null" ]; then
913	                    COST_BEFORE_CYCLE=$(echo "$BLOCKS_DATA" | jq -r --arg start "$WEEKLY_START_ISO" --arg end "$DAILY_CYCLE_START_ISO" '
914	                        [.blocks[] |
915	                         select(
916	                           (.startTime >= $start) and
917	                           (.startTime < $end)
918	                         ) |
919	                         .costUSD
920	                        ] | add // 0
921	                    ')
922	
923	                    # Weekly available at current daily cycle start
924	                    WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $WEEKLY_LIMIT - $COST_BEFORE_CYCLE}")
925	                    WEEKLY_AVAIL_PCT=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START / $WEEKLY_LIMIT) * 100}")
926	
927	                    # Calculate cycles left from now to NEXT reset
928	                    CURRENT_TIME=$(date +%s)
929	                    TIME_UNTIL_RESET=$((NEXT_RESET - CURRENT_TIME))
930	                    CYCLES_LEFT=$(awk "BEGIN {hours = $TIME_UNTIL_RESET / 3600; cycles = hours / 24; print (cycles == int(cycles)) ? int(cycles) : int(cycles) + 1}")
931	
932	                    if [ "$CYCLES_LEFT" -gt 0 ]; then
933	                        WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $WEEKLY_AVAIL_PCT / $CYCLES_LEFT}")
934	                    else
935	                        WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="0"
936	                    fi
937	                else
938	                    # Fallback to usage if data unavailable
939	                    WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="$WEEKLY_PCT"
940	                fi
941	            else
942	                # Fallback to usage if data unavailable
943	                WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="$WEEKLY_PCT"
944	            fi
945	            WEEKLY_LABEL="recom"
946	            ;;
947	        *)
948	            # Default: usage mode
949	            WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE="$WEEKLY_PCT"
950	            WEEKLY_LABEL="weekly"
951	            ;;
952	    esac
953	
954	    # Match color with daily section if daily is enabled, otherwise use layer logic
955	    if [ "$SHOW_DAILY" = "true" ] && [ -n "${DAILY_BAR_COLOR:-}" ]; then
956	        # Use same color as daily section
957	        WEEKLY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_BAR_COLOR")
958	    else
959	        # Apply layer logic directly to weekly percentage using static base
960	        WEEKLY_STATIC_BASE=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", ($WEEKLY_LIMIT / 7.0) / $WEEKLY_LIMIT * 100}")
961	        WEEKLY_LAYER1=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.2f\", $WEEKLY_STATIC_BASE * $DAILY_LAYER1_THRESHOLD_MULT}")
962	
963	        if (( $(awk "BEGIN {print ($WEEKLY_PCT <= $WEEKLY_LAYER1)}") )); then
964	            WEEKLY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_LAYER1_COLOR")
965	        else
966	            WEEKLY_COLOR=$(get_color_code "$DAILY_LAYER2_COLOR")
967	        fi
968	    fi
969	fi
970	
971	# ====================================================================================
972	# MONTHLY COST SECTION (independent)
973	# ====================================================================================
974	if [ "$SHOW_MONTHLY" = "true" ] && [ -n "$PAYMENT_CYCLE_START_DATE" ] && type get_monthly_cost &>/dev/null; then
975	    # Convert payment cycle start date to Unix timestamp
976	    PAYMENT_CYCLE_TIMESTAMP=$(iso_to_timestamp "$PAYMENT_CYCLE_START_DATE")
977	
978	    # Get monthly cost with caching
979	    MONTHLY_COST=$(get_monthly_cost "$PAYMENT_CYCLE_TIMESTAMP" "$CACHE_DURATION")
980	
981	    # Format monthly cost display
982	    if [ -n "$MONTHLY_COST" ] && [ "$MONTHLY_COST" != "0" ]; then
983	        MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $MONTHLY_COST}")
984	    fi
985	fi
986	
987	# ====================================================================================
988	# SESSIONS SECTION (independent)
989	# ====================================================================================
990	if [ "$SHOW_SESSIONS" = "true" ]; then
991	    # Count concurrent Claude Code sessions (projects with activity in last N minutes)
992	    # Expand tilde in path
993	    PROJECTS_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECTS_PATH/#\~/$HOME}"
994	    ACTIVE_SESSIONS=$(find "$PROJECTS_PATH" -name "*.jsonl" -type f -mmin -$SESSION_ACTIVITY_THRESHOLD 2>/dev/null | \
995	        xargs -I {} dirname {} 2>/dev/null | sort -u | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
996	fi
997	
998	# ====================================================================================
999	# STAGE 3: RENDERING - FINAL ASSEMBLY
1000	# ====================================================================================
1001	# Assemble all computed sections into the final statusline output.
1002	# Each section has already completed its own data→compute→render pipeline.
1003	# This stage conditionally includes sections based on:
1004	#   - Section toggle settings (show_*)
1005	#   - Data availability (presence of computed values)
1006	# ====================================================================================
1007	
1008	STATUSLINE_SECTIONS=()
1009	
1010	# Directory section
1011	[[ "$SHOW_DIRECTORY" == "true" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${ORANGE_CODE}${DIR_NAME}${RESET_CODE}")
1012	
1013	# Model section
1014	[[ -n "${MODEL_SHORT:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DIM_BLUE_CODE}${MODEL_SHORT}${RESET_CODE}")
1015	
1016	[[ "$SHOW_CONTEXT" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${CTX_TOTAL:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${CTX_COLOR}${CTX_TOTAL} ${CTX_PROGRESS_BAR}${RESET_CODE}")
1017	[[ "$SHOW_FIVE_HOUR_WINDOW" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${COST_FMT:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${PROGRESS_COLOR}${COST_FMT} ${PROGRESS_BAR} ${COST_PERCENTAGE}%${RESET_CODE}")
1018	
1019	# Add daily/weekly combined or separate sections
1020	if [[ "$SHOW_DAILY" == "true" ]] && [[ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" == "true" ]] && [[ "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" == "recommend" ]] && [[ -n "${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR:-}" ]] && [[ -n "${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE:-}" ]]; then
1021	    # Combined mode: daily [bar] actual/recommend% $actual/$recommend (with dimmed portions)
1022	    DIM_CODE="\033[2m"
1023	
1024	    # Calculate recommend daily cost from precise dollar amount (not rounded percentage)
1025	    # This avoids rounding error: $850/7 = $121 (not 14% × $850 = $119)
1026	    if [ -n "${WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START:-}" ] && [ -n "${CYCLES_LEFT:-}" ] && [ "$CYCLES_LEFT" -gt 0 ]; then
1027	        RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $WEEKLY_AVAIL_AT_CYCLE_START / $CYCLES_LEFT}")
1028	    else
1029	        # Fallback to percentage-based calculation
1030	        RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", ($WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE / 100) * $WEEKLY_LIMIT}")
1031	    fi
1032	
1033	    DAILY_COST_DISPLAY=$(awk "BEGIN {printf \"%.0f\", $DAILY_COST}")
1034	
1035	    # Conditionally show "daily " label
1036	    if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1037	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}daily ${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}${DIM_CODE}/${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}% \$${DAILY_COST_DISPLAY}/\$${RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST}${RESET_CODE}")
1038	    else
1039	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}${DIM_CODE}/${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}% \$${DAILY_COST_DISPLAY}/\$${RECOMMEND_DAILY_COST}${RESET_CODE}")
1040	    fi
1041	elif [[ "$SHOW_DAILY" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR:-}" ]]; then
1042	    # Daily only
1043	    if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1044	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}daily ${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}%${RESET_CODE}")
1045	    else
1046	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DAILY_COLOR}${DAILY_PROGRESS_BAR} ${DAILY_PCT_DISPLAY}%${RESET_CODE}")
1047	    fi
1048	fi
1049	
1050	# Weekly section (only if not already combined with daily)
1051	if [[ "$SHOW_WEEKLY" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE:-}" ]]; then
1052	    if [[ "$SHOW_DAILY" != "true" ]] || [[ "$WEEKLY_DISPLAY_MODE" != "recommend" ]]; then
1053	        # Conditionally show weekly label
1054	        if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1055	            STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${WEEKLY_COLOR}${WEEKLY_LABEL} ${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}%${RESET_CODE}")
1056	        else
1057	            STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${WEEKLY_COLOR}${WEEKLY_DISPLAY_VALUE}%${RESET_CODE}")
1058	        fi
1059	    fi
1060	fi
1061	
1062	# Monthly cost section (between weekly and timer)
1063	if [[ "$SHOW_MONTHLY" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY:-}" ]]; then
1064	    # Conditionally show "total " label
1065	    if [[ "$SHOW_LABELS" == "true" ]]; then
1066	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DIM_BLUE_CODE}total \$${MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY}${RESET_CODE}")
1067	    else
1068	        STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${DIM_BLUE_CODE}\$${MONTHLY_COST_DISPLAY}${RESET_CODE}")
1069	    fi
1070	fi
1071	
1072	[[ "$SHOW_TIMER" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${RESET_INFO:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${PURPLE_CODE}${RESET_INFO}${RESET_CODE}")
1073	[[ "$SHOW_TOKEN_RATE" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${TOKEN_RATE:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${CYAN_CODE}${TOKEN_RATE}${RESET_CODE}")
1074	[[ "$SHOW_SESSIONS" == "true" ]] && [[ -n "${ACTIVE_SESSIONS:-}" ]] && STATUSLINE_SECTIONS+=("${CYAN_CODE}×${ACTIVE_SESSIONS}${RESET_CODE}")
1075	
1076	# Join sections with separator
1077	# Define dimmed separator
1078	DIM_CODE="\033[2m"
1079	SEPARATOR=" ${DIM_CODE}|${RESET_CODE} "
1080	
1081	STATUSLINE=""
1082	FIRST=true
1083	for section in "${STATUSLINE_SECTIONS[@]}"; do
1084	    if [[ "$FIRST" == "true" ]]; then
1085	        STATUSLINE="$section"
1086	        FIRST=false
1087	    else
1088	        STATUSLINE="$STATUSLINE$SEPARATOR$section"
1089	    fi
1090	done
1091	
1092	# Display statusline
1093	if [ -n "$STATUSLINE" ]; then
1094	    printf '%b\n' "$STATUSLINE"
1095	else
1096	    # Fallback if no sections enabled
1097	    echo "$DIR_NAME | No active window"
1098	fi
1099

content.text:
The answer is right there in the TIMER section. Here is what "11:34AM/3PM (4h 26m)" means:

**The format is: `[current time] / [reset time] ([time remaining])`**

- **11:34AM** — the current time at the moment the statusline was rendered (formatted as `%-l:%M%p`, i.e. hour with minutes and AM/PM).

- **3PM** — the time when the current 5-hour usage window resets, i.e. when ccusage's active billing block ends (formatted as `%-l%p`, hour only with AM/PM, no minutes). This comes from the `endTime` field of the active ccusage block.

- **(4h 26m)** — the time remaining until that reset, i.e. how long is left in the current 5-hour window. This is derived from ccusage's `projection.remainingMinutes` field.

In plain terms: at 11:34 in the morning, your current 5-hour Claude usage window will reset at 3PM, and you have 4 hours and 26 minutes left before that happens. The secondary parts (`/3PM (4h 26m)`) are rendered in dimmed text to visually de-emphasise them relative to the current time.

---

For any further changes to your status line, use the **statusline-setup** agent (the one that manages `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline.sh` and `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json`). You can ask Claude to update the status line display at any time and it will invoke that agent to make the changes.
